title: Avellino, Aiello: "In casa Juve diversi profili interessanti. Palumbo? Vorremmo tenerlo, ma ..."
excerpt: Fra Avellino e Juventus potrebbero esserci nuovi affari dopo quelli dello scorso anno legati al portiere Daffara e al centrocampista offensivo Palumbo.
summary: Fra Avellino e Juventus potrebbero esserci nuovi affari dopo quelli dello scorso anno legati al portiere Daffara e al centrocampista offensivo Palumbo. Il club irpino infatti ha messo nel mirino il centrocampista Giacomo Faticanti e gli attaccanti Alessandro Pietrelli ed Emanuele Pecorino e su questi nomi si è soffermato il direttore sportivo Mario Aiello ai microfoni di Tuttojuve.com: “Siamo alla ricerca di un centrocampista con determinate caratteristiche. Faticanti è uno dei profili apprezzati, ma stiamo valutando anche altre opportunità nello stesso reparto. Ne stiamo parlando con la Juventus, a noi farebbe piacere prenderlo se dovessero crearsi le giuste condizioni. Questo è un lavoro che portiamo avanti da tempo a livello di scouting, chiaramente stiamo cercando di capire quale può esser la miglior scelta per noi". Spazio poi agli altri profili che potrebbero interessare alla sua squadra: “Per Pecorino posso confermare l'interesse, è un giocatore su cui c'è la nostra attenzione. Sappiamo che è molto ambito per la categoria, ci sono dei club di primo livello con cui competere e che hanno degli obiettivi chiari. - prosegue ancora Aiello - Pietrelli era un'idea balenata all'inizio, ora l'abbiamo messa un po' in stand-by per una questione di modulo". Il ds irpino infine si sofferma su quel Martin Palumbo che piace a diversi club dopo un’ottima stagione in Serie B: “Ci auguriamo possa essere parte del nostro futuro, è arrivato in Serie C e quest’anno ha dimostrato di poter f...

title: Lukaku torna all'Anderlecht? Il nuovo tecnico: "Se avete il suo numero lo chiamo"
excerpt: Il nome di Romelu Lukaku continua a essere accostato all'Anderlecht, il club che lo ha lanciato nel calcio professionistico e dove, in Belgio, molti immaginano possa concludere la propria carriera.
summary: Il nome di Romelu Lukaku continua a essere accostato all'Anderlecht, il club che lo ha lanciato nel calcio professionistico e dove, in Belgio, molti immaginano possa concludere la propria carriera. Lo stesso attaccante del Napoli, in più occasioni, ha ammesso di voler tornare un giorno a vestire quella maglia, pur ribadendo recentemente che non è ancora arrivato il momento. L'argomento è tornato d'attualità nel corso della conferenza stampa di presentazione del nuovo allenatore dell'Anderlecht, Vitor Bruno. Al tecnico portoghese è stato chiesto di commentare l'eventualità di rivedere il centravanti belga nel club biancomalva, con cui ha giocato tra settore giovanile e prima squadra dal 2009 al 2011. La risposta di Vitor Bruno è stata affidata a una battuta, che ha strappato un sorriso ai presenti: "Se avete il suo numero, lo chiamo".

title: Palestra set for Chelsea medical and contract signing tomorrow
excerpt: Marco Palestra is set to fly to London in order to sign the paperwork and...
summary: Marco Palestra is set to fly to London in order to sign the paperwork and undergo his medical ahead of his transfer from Atalanta to Premier League side Chelsea. According to reports from Sky, via FCInter1908, Palestra will now travel to England to sign what is expected to be a six-year contract with the Blues. Chelsea came almost out of nowhere to beat Inter to the signing of the highly rated Italian wing-back, who had a stellar season with Cagliari and was named Serie A defender of the season.

title: World Cup 2026: Curacao vs Ivory Coast lineups – Atalanta’s Kossounou and Inter talent Bonny start for Ivorians
excerpt: Curacao vs Ivory Coast kicks off this evening, with both sides’ future in the FIFA...
summary: Curacao vs Ivory Coast kicks off this evening, with both sides’ future in the FIFA World Cup still very much hanging in the balance. Ivory Coast’s three points put them in a commanding position in second, with two points gap between themselves and Curacao and Ecuador. Curacao, meanwhile, battled hard for a draw against Ecuador, meaning a shock victory over their opposition today would probably be enough for them to move second – barring any surprises from Ecuador against Germany.

title: World Cup 2026: Ecuador vs Germany lineups – Venezia’s Yeboah starts for South Americans as Estupinan benched
excerpt: Ecuador vs Germany is one of this evening’s two World Cup Group E deciders, with...
summary: Ecuador vs Germany is one of this evening’s two World Cup Group E deciders, with the European side already through into the next round and the South Americans with just a point to their name so far. Ecuador have largely disappointed and are yet to score a single goal, losing 1-0 to Ivory Coast before a goalless draw with Curacao. Germany, meanwhile, thumped Curacao 7-1 before a comeback 2-1 victory over Ivory Coast.

title: Napoli want to reunite Allegri with Gatti as Juventus set asking price
excerpt: Napoli are targeting Federico Gatti as one of Massimiliano Allegri’s first defensive signings, with the...
summary: Napoli are targeting Federico Gatti as one of Massimiliano Allegri’s first defensive signings, with the incoming coach understood to want the Juventus centre-back at his disposal once his appointment is formally confirmed following the resolution of his contract dispute with Milan. According to SportMediaset, via TuttoMercatoWeb, the deal could be concluded for approximately €20 million, a figure that would represent a significant profit for Juventus given Gatti’s original transfer fee from Frosinone. The defender worked closely with Allegri at Juventus and the coach’s desire to reunite with him in Naples is understood to be a genuine factor in the pursuit.

title: Spalletti wants Milinkovic-Savic as Juventus and Napoli searches intertwine
excerpt: A fascinating goalkeeper triangle has emerged between Napoli, Juventus and Tottenham, with Luciano Spalletti’s personal...
summary: A fascinating goalkeeper triangle has emerged between Napoli, Juventus and Tottenham, with Luciano Spalletti’s personal admiration for Vanja Milinkovic-Savic creating a direct link between two clubs who are both rebuilding between the posts this summer. According to Corriere dello Sport, via IlNapolista, Juventus are monitoring Milinkovic-Savic’s situation at Napoli with genuine interest, the Serbian’s ability with the ball at his feet making him an attractive fit for Spalletti’s system. Napoli, meanwhile, have decided to sell the goalkeeper after just one season, with Allegri understood to have identified Guglielmo Vicario as his preferred number one for the new era.

title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
excerpt: With the final round of group fixtures under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.
summary: Japan need a point against Sweden on Thursday to secure an automatic place in the last 32 With the final round of group fixtures now under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape. But with 32 of the 48 teams advancing to the knockout rounds, new tie-breaker rules, and a table of third-placed teams to keep track of, staying across the various permutations is far from simple.

title: The games that show the flaws in a 48-team World Cup
excerpt: Two matches in the final round of group games present the chance for two teams to simply play out a draw to qualify.
summary: It takes 72 games to reduce the number of teams at the World Cup from 48 to 32 The World Cup has, so far, lacked an element of real jeopardy in the group stage. Granted, South Korea may disagree after a shock 1-0 defeat by South Africa on Wednesday sent Bafana Bafana into the knockout stage for the first time.

title: Why it's time for Saka and Rashford - Shearer
excerpt: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why Thomas Tuchel needs to change some personnel as well as his team's mindset when they face Panama.
summary: History shows us why there is no need to panic about England's draw with Ghana on Tuesday, but also underlines why it is important we immediately bounce back. Since 2006, every World Cup winner has dropped points in their first or second group game - but what they have also done each time is go on to win their group. I am confident England will do that too, by beating Panama on Saturday, but I would not be surprised if Thomas Tuchel makes three or even four changes to his team after such a disappointing result and performance.

title: Double Ballon d'Or winner Putellas to join London City Lionesses
excerpt: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.
summary: Alexia Putellas won four Champions League titles with Barcelona Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas has opted to join Women's Super League club London City Lionesses. The 32-year-old left Barcelona after 14 years in May and is widely regarded as one of women's football's greatest players.

title: Stay or go home? Tartan Army in limbo as Scotland await fate
excerpt: BBC Scotland examines options for the Tartan Army in Miami as Scotland wait to discover if their World Cup adventure will continue or not.
summary: Scotland fans have seen their team score once in three group games Scotland's World Cup progression hangs in the balance, with multiple favours needed to make it out of Group C. Travelling fans may not know the outcome until Saturday night, so what are they going to do until then?

title: The great paradox of Scotland’s World Cup. The fans, superb. The team? Dismal | Ewan Murray
excerpt:
A mess against Brazil, Steve Clarke’s side are in purgatory, hanging around as a faint hope of a place in the last 32 lives on

The sad thing is that in a matter of weeks, this will all have been forgotten about. The intensely tribal nature of Scotland’s football domain means that a new dome
summary: A mess against Brazil, Steve Clarke’s side are in purgatory, hanging around as a faint hope of a place in the last 32 lives on The sad thing is that in a matter of weeks, this will all have been forgotten about. The intensely tribal nature of Scotland’s football domain means that a new domestic campaign will lead to scratching, swearing and howling that will dominate for months on end. Some may argue it will be wise to banish thoughts of Scottish participation in this World Cup. It should, in truth, serve as a much-needed line in the sporting sand. The psychological, societal and commercial benefits to Scotland of World Cup participation have been borne out in recent weeks. Not only has the tournament captured hearts and minds in Scotland but the Tartan Army has done likewise across the United States. Scottish people, self-deprecating as standard, should take a morale boost from football supporters acting as such wonderful ambassadors for their country.

title: Erling Haaland v Kylian Mbappé: how the Norway and France hitmen match up
excerpt:
Two of the best forwards in the game face each other in the World Cup on Friday. Their most dramatic contrast is in international football

• Golden Boot: World Cup 202
summary: Two of the best forwards in the game face each other in the World Cup on Friday. Their most dramatic contrast is in international football Golden Boot: World Cup 2026 top goalscorers All-time World Cup goalscorers Norway v France is arguably the most talent-loaded fixture in the World Cup group stage. Transfermarkt’s combined estimated market value of the two squads is higher than for any of the other 71 matches in this phase of the tournament. Yet the vast majority of the attention at Boston Stadium will be focused on just two men: Erling Haaland and Kylian Mbappé, who are already in the leading pack to win the World Cup Golden Boot for top goalscorers, both scoring twice in each of their first two games.

title: The difference between Gareth Southgate and Thomas Tuchel is not that simple | Cath Bishop
excerpt:
The assumption that the England coach gave his team a rocket against Croatia at half-time is wrong. In fact he did the opposite

We’ve completed the Southgate leadership lessons and now we find ourselves at the beginning of the Tuchelosophy course. We can already see some of the key modules
summary: The assumption that the England coach gave his team a rocket against Croatia at half-time is wrong. In fact he did the opposite We’ve completed the Southgate leadership lessons and now we find ourselves at the beginning of the Tuchelosophy course. We can already see some of the key modules we’ll be studying over the next few weeks. But it’s important we’re ready to learn with open minds and ditch some of the old tropes. The dominant simplistic narrative that accompanied the shift from Gareth Southgate to Thomas Tuchel was that the former wasn’t ruthless enough and therefore the latter will be more ruthless. There are already assumptions and interpretations of Tuchel’s actions and words being made through that lens which need challenging.

title: Hearts appoint Vrancken as McInnes' replacement
excerpt: Hearts have appointed Wouter Vrancken as their new head coach.
summary: Hearts name Wouter Vrancken as their new head coach; Belgian replaces Derek McInnes, who left after last season's second-place finish in the Scottish Premiership to join Rangers; Vrancken won the Belgian Cup and was a runner-up in the Belgian Pro League Hearts have appointed Wouter Vrancken as their new head coach. The Belgian replaces Derek McInnes, who left to join Rangers earlier this month.

title: England stage mini fightback after draining first day at Trent Bridge
excerpt: England staged a mini fightback deep into an otherwise demoralising, draining first day of the series deciding third Test against New Zealand at a scorching Trent Bridge.
summary: Tom Latham (151) and Devon Conway (157) share in New Zealand record-opening partnership of 317 vs England; hosts take four wickets in final session - including two in two balls to end day; watch day two live on Sky Sports Cricket from 10.15am, Friday (first ball 11am) Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England staged a mini fightback deep into an otherwise demoralising, draining first day of the series deciding third Test against New Zealand at a scorching Trent Bridge.

title: Tennis great Evert undergoes surgery as cancer returns
excerpt: Tennis legend Chris Evert has undergone surgery following the return of her ovarian cancer and will not attend Wimbledon this year.
summary: Eighteen-time Grand Slam singles champion Chris Evert, 71, first diagnosed with cancer in 2021; American will begin chemotherapy in coming weeks; Evert says: "I am deeply grateful to my medical team, my family, friends and everyone who has reached out with kindness and encouragement" Tennis legend Chris Evert has undergone surgery following the return of her ovarian cancer and will not attend Wimbledon this year. The American won 18 Grand Slam singles titles between 1974 and 1986, with only Serena Williams (23) and Steffi Graf (22) claiming more women's singles majors in the Open era.

title: Putellas decides to join London City Lionesses
excerpt: Alexia Putellas has decided to London City Lionesses on a free transfer following her departure from Barcelona.
summary: Alexia Putellas has decided to move to the WSL with London City Lionesses; she left Barcelona after 14 years with the club; Putellas has won two Ballon d'Ors and is the favourite to win it again this year; she also has numerous Champions League and Primera Division titles Alexia Putellas has decided to London City Lionesses on a free transfer following her departure from Barcelona. The Spain international was understood to be happy with the terms offered by the WSL club a few weeks ago, but wanted to explore interest from the NWSL.

title: Guy Stéphan: "No me veo en mi sitio en la sala de prensa"
excerpt: El segundo de Deschamps va a dirigir a Francia ante Noruega ante la ausencia del seleccionador
summary: El segundo de Deschamps va a dirigir a Francia ante Noruega ante la ausencia del seleccionador Guy Stéphan ha dado la rueda de prensa previa al partido ante Noruega ante la ausencia de DidierDeschamps, que abandonó hace dos días la concentración por el fallecimiento de su madre y que no se sentará en el banquillo del Boston Stadium en el tercer partido del grupo y que decide el liderato. En el banquillo ante Noruerga: "Todo mi apoyo a Deschamps y a su familia. Es un momento complicado. Didier me ha llamado pronto por la mañana y me ha dado en su habitación la mala noticia. Me ha dicho que me encargue del grupo. Me voy a esforzar y voy a tratar de tratar de estar a la altura y corresponder a la confianza que me ha dado".

title: Ecuador - Alemania en directo
Tchouaméni: "Si te despistas con Haaland lo pagas caro"
excerpt: El jugador del Real Madrid regresa al once con Francia tras su suplencia frente a Irak
summary: El jugador del Real Madrid regresa al once con Francia tras su suplencia frente a Irak Aurélien Tchouaméni ha dado la rueda de prensa en la Universidad de Bentley, campo base de Francia en Boston, antes de medirse mañana a Noruega. El jugador del Real Madrid destaca a Haaland como la gran amenaza y cree que Kylian, más allá de su buen momento, siempre aparece en los grandes momentos. Recuerdo para Deschamps: "En nombre de todos quiero transmitir el apoyo al entrenador, su familia y amigos, es un momento difícil pero nos ha dado un objetivo, que ganemos, y vamos a hacer todo lo posible".

title: México se suma al selecto club de las selecciones con inicios perfectos
excerpt: Es la cuarta que, desde 1958, consigue ganar sus tres primeros duelos sin conceder gol
summary: Es la cuarta que, desde 1958, consigue ganar sus tres primeros duelos sin conceder gol México vive días de felicidad, hasta el punto de entrar en un club muy selecto en la historia de los Mundiales, aquel en el que después de tres encuentros de la fase inicial de grupos los ha completado todos con victoria y sin conceder goles a los rivales, siendo la cuarta de la historia que lo consigue, con Argentina con opciones de igualar y repetir. La selección dirigida por Javier Aguirre inauguró el Mundial a lo grande en el Azteca con una victoria por 2-0 frente a Sudáfrica, ganó por 1-0 a Corea del Sur para convertirse en la primera selección clasificada para los dieciseisavos de final y completó una primera fase perfecta con la goleada frente a la República Checa por 3-0. Una primera fase soñada para cualquier equipo y más para un anfitrión.

title: El Huesca rebaja un 19% los abonos y anuncia el regreso de Tresaco
excerpt: El extremo es el octavo fichaje para esta próxima temporada
summary: El extremo es el octavo fichaje para esta próxima temporada Mañana movida en el Huesca. El club ha presentado su campaña de abonados y ha hecho oficial el regreso de Rafa Tresaco, que se convierte en el octavo fichaje de la temporada. El tema de la campaña han sido los “15.628 km²” que son la superficie total de la provincia de Huesca y representa a todos los aficionados azulgranas. La principal novedad tras el descenso es la reducción del precio de los abonos en todas las categorías y en todas las zonas del estadio, con una bajada media del 19%. El abono tiene un coste medio de 9 euros por partido. Además, la Copa del Rey estará incluida en el abono, incluso si el rival fuera un equipo de Primera, y los posibles playoffs de ascenso.

title: Modena, dopo Massolin arriva Manquant: la Serie B come trampolino
excerpt: Il Modena ha un metodo.
summary: Il Modena ha un metodo. E funziona. Pochi mesi dopo aver ceduto Yanis Massolin all'Inter per una cifra base di 3,5 milioni di euro (acquistato appena sei mesi prima per circa 900mila euro dai belgi del Francs Borains) il club emiliano ha già messo a segno il primo colpo dell'estate: Joris Manquant, attaccante francese classe 2005, arriva a parametro zero dallo Stade Nyonnais dopo la scadenza del contratto. Chi è Joris Manquant Nato a Lione il 20 ottobre 2005, Manquant è cresciuto nel settore giovanile dell'Olympique Lione e ha avuto esperienze formative anche al Metz. Con i suoi 190 centimetri ha la struttura fisica di una prima punta, ma le caratteristiche tecniche di un attaccante moderno, elegante e mobile. Nell'ultima stagione con lo Stade Nyonnais ha totalizzato 29 presenze in Challenge League, con 13 gol e 3 assist tra campionato e coppa. Battuta la concorrenza europea Sul giovane attaccante si erano mosse anche squadre come Braga, AZ Alkmaar e Heerenveen, ma il Modena ha anticipato tutti assicurandosi uno dei profili più promettenti dell'intera Challenge League svizzera. La scelta di approdare in Serie B, piuttosto che in Olanda o Portogallo, dice molto sull'attrattività del progetto costruito dalla società guidata dal presidente Carlo Rivetti e dal lavoro di scouting di Andrea Catellani.

title: Perché anche il Frosinone ha deciso di cedere le quote agli americani
excerpt: Ci siamo: secondo quanto raccolto da TMW, la prossima sarà la settimana decisiva per il passaggio di quote al vertice del club ciociaro.
summary: Ci siamo: secondo quanto raccolto da TMW, la prossima sarà la settimana decisiva per il passaggio di quote al vertice del club ciociaro. Dopo oltre vent'anni di gestione targata famiglia Stirpe, entrerà infatti in società Gamechanger 20, holding britannica già protagonista nel 2021 dell'acquisizione dell'Ipswich Town. Alle spalle dell'operazione c'è il supporto finanziario di ORG, società con sede in Arizona che amministra importanti fondi pensione statunitensi, tra cui quelli di polizia e vigili del fuoco. Figura centrale dell'affare è l'imprenditore americano Brett Johnson. Perché la famiglia Stirpe ha deciso di cedere una parte sostanziale delle quote del club del Frosinone agli americani? Quali sono le prospettive? Che ruolo avrà Stirpe? Chi sono gli uomini di Gamechanger 20? Ne parliamo nel Podcast di oggi di Tuttomercatoweb.com in compagnia di Marco Conterio

title: Thuram via dalla Juve? Servono 40 milioni. Sondaggio Nottingham, lo valuta il PSG
excerpt: Khephren Thuram è una delle pedine sacrificabili da una Juventus che ha necessità anche di vendere per rinforzare la rosa e assecondare le richieste dell'allenatore Luciano Spalletti.
summary: Khephren Thuram è una delle pedine sacrificabili da una Juventus che ha necessità anche di vendere per rinforzare la rosa e assecondare le richieste dell'allenatore Luciano Spalletti. Dalla prossima settimana entrerà in vigore la clausola del difensore brasiliano Gleison Bremer e chissà che qualche club di Premier League non possa pensare di pagarla per portare il giocatore in Inghilterra. Il centrale ora a disposizione di Ancelotti è uno di quei calciatori con cui la Juventus potrebbe fare plusvalenza: un altro è Andrea Cambiaso, un altro ancora Khepren Thuram che in questi giorni sta osservando davanti alla tv il cammino di suoi fratello e di tutta la nazionale francese ai Mondiali. Thuram nella Juventus di Spalletti non è stato una pedina fondamentale. Titolare certo, ma nei ragionamenti tra valutazione di mercato e importanza sul terreno di gioco lui è uno di quelli che la società bianconera può cedere. Ovviamente al giusto prezzo, in questo caso quantificabile intorno ai quaranta milioni di euro. Classe 2001, 25 anni compiuti lo scorso 26 marzo, Thuram due anni fa fu acquistato per circa 20 milioni di euro. I numeri dicono che la sua prima stagione in bianconero è stata migliore della seconda dove, comunque, ha collezionato più di quaranta presenze. Ha realizzato quattro gol. Sono dati da calciatore importante che hanno smosso il mercato europeo, anche se per ora nessuna offerta all'altezza delle aspettative della Juve e del ragazzo. Fin qui il sondaggio più concreto è s...

title: USA, Balogun: "Quasi frustrante vedere giocatori così forti. Vorrei essere come loro"
excerpt: Folarin Balogun, attaccante degli Stati Uniti, ha parlato oggi alla Gazzetta dello Sport della propria esperienza al Mondiale di casa: "La mia storia è speciale e unica, tutti sanno che avrei potuto giocare anche per l’Inghilterra, dove ho sempre vissuto,...
summary: Folarin Balogun, attaccante degli Stati Uniti, ha parlato oggi alla Gazzetta dello Sport della propria esperienza al Mondiale di casa: "La mia storia è speciale e unica, tutti sanno che avrei potuto giocare anche per l’Inghilterra, dove ho sempre vissuto, e per la Nigeria, ma sono orgoglioso di aver scelto l’America: l’entusiasmo e l’ambizione di questo popolo mi ispirano, qui ci sono infinite possibilità. Ora, però, voglio scrivere la storia di questa nazionale e segnare altri gol, perché quelli del Mondiale sono regali che ti restano per sempre. Ancora più importante è provare a vincere la terza gara su tre, poi penseremo al resto del torneo…". Definisce l'esperienza al Mondiale come fantastica, se la sta godendo in pieno e gli piace che la gente inizi a crederci davvero, come la squadra. McKennie gli mostra di continuo video di tifosi che guardano le partite nei bar o su maxischermi in varie parti del Paese e, quando segnano, tutti impazziscono Messi, Mbappé, Haaland hanno numeri inavvicinabili: "È quasi frustrante vedere giocatori così forti… Sono una vera sentenza, segnano una rete a partita, a volte anche di più. Vorrei arrivare a quel livello, essere costante come loro. Sono convinto di avere il potenziale per avvicinarmi".

title: Spezia, Angelozzi: "Rapporto viscerale con la città. Sono felice anche in Serie C"
excerpt: All'indomani della conferenza stampa con la quale si è ripresentato a La Spezia come direttore sportivo nell'anno della Serie C, Guido Angelozzi ha rilasciato, sul medesimo tema, alcune dichiarazioni in esclusiva ai microfoni di TuttoMercartoWeb.
summary: All'indomani della conferenza stampa con la quale si è ripresentato a La Spezia come direttore sportivo nell'anno della Serie C, Guido Angelozzi ha rilasciato, sul medesimo tema, alcune dichiarazioni in esclusiva ai microfoni di TuttoMercartoWeb.com, all'interno della rubrica 'A Tu per Tu': Direttore, è tornato allo Spezia. Sensazioni? "Ho un rapporto viscerale con questa città, sono felice di essere tornato. Questa è una piazza che mi stimola, la società mi ha voluto". Però uno come lei in Serie C.... "L'importante è andare dove si ha la possibilità di lavorare. Per me non fa differenza la categoria, ma poter lavorare e potersi esprimere".

title: Palestra to Chelsea: What Does It Mean for Italy? Has He Betrayed Inter? Video
excerpt: A new episode of the Football Italia Summer Show is out on YouTube with Lorenzo...
summary: A new episode of the Football Italia Summer Show is out on YouTube with Lorenzo Bettoni and Richard Hall discussing Marco Palestra’s imminent move to Chelsea. Palestra has chosen Chelsea over Inter and is now set to complete a permanent move worth more than €50m to Stamford Bridge. For weeks, Inter had been working on a deal with Atalanta and appeared to be leading the race for one of Italy’s brightest young talents. However, Chelsea’s financial power and long-term project have ultimately convinced the player to make the move to the Premier League.

title: Roma have buyers willing to spend €40m but Soule unconvinced by Saudi Arabia
excerpt: Reports in Italy claim that several Saudi Pro League sides are willing to invest €40m...
summary: Reports in Italy claim that several Saudi Pro League sides are willing to invest €40m on the signing of Matias Soule from Roma, but the Argentine is reportedly unconvinced by the idea of moving away from Europe at this stage of his career. It is no secret that Roma are looking to sell players before the end of the month in order to raise funds to comply with their financial fair play settlement agreement with UEFA. Several of the Giallorossi’s star players, including the likes of Manu Kone and Evan Ndicka, have previously been linked with a move away from the Stadio Olimpico in order to raise the required funds, but reports over the last few weeks indicate that Soule is increasingly likely to be sold if an appropriate offer comes in.

title: Atalanta teach a lesson in sales – €100m from Man United and Chelsea before July
excerpt: Atalanta continue to stake their claim as the finest selling club in Italy, as La...
summary: Atalanta continue to stake their claim as the finest selling club in Italy, as La Dea are set to bring in over €100m from sales to Chelsea and Manchester United, before the summer transfer window has even opened. The Serie A transfer window will officially open on June 29, but Atalanta have already secured a total €60m deal for Marco Palestra to join Chelsea this summer, while midfielder Ederson is all set to join Manchester United in a €45m deal – both figures including bonuses. There have even been reports over the last 24 hours that Brighton & Hove Albion are interested in a deal for 18-year-old defender Honest Ahanor, which could push the figure of €105m even higher.

title: Inter could reinvest Palestra funds on Nico Paz from Real Madrid instead
excerpt: Inter were left disappointed by Marco Palestra’s decision to join Chelsea earlier this week, but...
summary: Inter were left disappointed by Marco Palestra’s decision to join Chelsea earlier this week, but the latest reports claim that the Nerazzurri could now reinvest the funds they had set aside for the wing-back on Nico Paz from Real Madrid instead. Denzel Dumfries is set to join Real Madrid from Inter having had his release clause activated earlier this month, and the Nerazzurri had been hoping to replace him with 21-year-old Italy talent Palestra. However, the 2025-26 Cagliari loanee has since decided to join Premier League outfit Chelsea in a total €60m deal on a five or six-year contract that will see him earn twice as much as he was offered in Italy in wages.

title: Brighton express interest in signing Italy U21 talent Ahanor from Atalanta
excerpt: Reports in Italy claim that Premier League side Brighton and Hove Albion have registered an...
summary: Reports in Italy claim that Premier League side Brighton and Hove Albion have registered an interest in signing Atalanta and Azzurrini talent Honest Ahanor this summer. According to reports from Gianluca Di Marzio of Sky Sport in Italy, representatives from Brighton have been in Milan over the last few days for a series of meetings, and in that time, the topic of signing Ahanor from Atalanta has come up in conversations. No formal negotiations have taken place as things stand, and Di Marzio suggests that it will not be straightforward to haggle with Atalanta this summer. That is because La Dea are not pressed to sell players to raise funds given that they are set to sell Marco Palestra to Chelsea in a €60m package and Ederson to Manchester United for a total sum of €45m.

title: Vinicius Jr stops fun and leaves Scots down... but are they out?
excerpt: Scotland's party stops at the hands of Brazil in Miami, but might they still find themselves in the last 32?
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil God bless the Tartan Army in all their beery magnificence. God bless them for their noise and colour and all-round cuddliness. God bless them for charming the good people of Boston and Miami and earning a full page feature in Wednesday's O Globo, Brazil's best-selling newspaper. They tried to lift their team in Miami but a forklift truck couldn't have got Scotland's challenge off the ground - not while they defended as they did early on, not while they only stirred and forced five saves from Alisson in the Brazil goal when they were already 3-0 down.

title: What do Scotland need to make World Cup knockouts?
excerpt: After their 3-0 defeat by Brazil, BBC Sport looks at what Scotland require to reach the knockout phase.
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil Reaching the last 32 of the World Cup is out of Scotland's hands after they suffered a 3-0 defeat by Brazil in their final group-stage game. Scotland fans will now be clinging on to hopes of progressing as one of the eight best third-placed sides.

title: French footballer Kies, 21, dies from drowning
excerpt: French footballer Kenzo Kies has died at the age of 21 after drowning in the Rhone river.
summary: Kenzo Kies came through the youth set-up at Saint-Etienne French footballer Kenzo Kies has died from drowning in the Rhone river. Kies, 21, who played for Guingamp in Ligue 2, was in a critical condition when he was pulled from the water by firefighters on Monday, and later died in hospital.

title: The Gakpo conundrum facing Liverpool
excerpt: Cody Gakpo is thriving with the Netherlands but increased competition means his Liverpool future is uncertain.
summary: Cody Gakpo has scored five World Cup goals in seven games for the Netherlands In the aftermath of scoring twice for the Netherlands against Sweden, Cody Gakpo was asked about his role for his country compared with how he is deployed at Liverpool. "A good question. Obviously it's a little bit different," Gakpo responded.

title: 'Think we're going home' - Clarke & McGinn rue Scotland mistakes
excerpt: Scotland will have to wait until potentially the early hours of Sunday to find out if their World Cup campaign will continue, but midfielder John McGinn suspects that is "unlikely" and head coach Steve Clarke suspects they are "going home".
summary: Scotland hopes on the brink following defeat against Brazil Scotland will have to wait until potentially the early hours of Sunday to find out if their World Cup campaign will continue, but midfielder John McGinn believes it is "unlikely" and head coach Steve Clarke suspects they are "going home". Knowing a draw would almost certainly be enough to reach the last 32, Steve Clarke's side suffered a chastening 3-0 defeat by Brazil to finish third in Group C.

title: World Cup 2026: Scotland on brink after Brazil defeat, South Africa advance, and more – live
excerpt:
⚽ Latest news from day 15 | Knockout permutations
⚽ W
summary: Football, famously, is a team game, something that appears to have eluded a certain type of pundit (and consumer). And Jonathan Liew has some wise words to say about this tournament’s intrusive cult of the individual: double quotation mark“Cristiano Ronaldo’s record-equalling sixth World Cup got off to a disappointing start,” began the Reuters match report of Portugal’s 1-1 draw against the Democratic Republic of the Congo last week. And yes, OK: everyone knows how this game works and why everyone plays it. On one hand, perhaps the greatest sporting day in the history of the world’s 15th most populous country. On the other, 41-year-old man does not score. It’s no contest, really. Get those sweet keywords front and left. Harvest that delicious search traffic. Perhaps you even noticed how I just did exactly the same thing. And yet something does feel qualitatively different this summer: a tectonic shift driven partly by events on the pitch and partly at the behest of the industry itself. This is a World Cup swimming in star names, and never have those star names been so unapologetically, unquestioningly invoked. France do not beat Iraq; instead Kylian Mbappé throws down the gauntlet to Erling Haaland, Harry Kane and the rest. According to Google, Miroslav Klose’s goals record has been searched more at this tournament than in the year he set it. At times the group phase has felt like an inconvenient distraction from the real business of the Golden Boot race. (Can Lionel Messi li...

title: Vinícius Júnior leads Brazil’s star cast in blockbuster show against sorry Scotland | Paul MacInnes
excerpt:
Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play – and on Wednesday one shone brighter than anyone else

“Water so clear you can see to the bottom, hundred thousand dollar cars e’ybody got ‘em. Ain’t no surprise in the club to see Sly Stallone, Miami my second home.” So said Will Smith
summary: Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play – and on Wednesday one shone brighter than anyone else “Water so clear you can see to the bottom, hundred thousand dollar cars e’ybody got ‘em. Ain’t no surprise in the club to see Sly Stallone, Miami my second home.” So said Will Smith, and the actor-rapper’s observations ring true to this day. Miami is still a city where the stars come out to play. On this night however, and unfortunately from a Scottish perspective, Vinícius Júnior came to play his way through their defence. Just as thousands of Scots colonised Boston, so it appeared that the entire upper middle class of São Paulo had come to Florida for this big game. Brazilian celebrities had followed too, the most obvious being Ronaldinho who not only officially signed for a third division Italian side while in the city, but was afforded a VVIP spot just outside the players’ tunnel where he could greet the players, and manager Carlo Ancelotti, as they made their way out.

title: Club World Cup likely to expand for 2029 with more Premier League clubs involved
excerpt:
• Fifa agrees joint venture with lobby group EFC

  • Next edition poised to expand from 32 to 48 clubs

Fifa has agreed to create a joint venture with the lobby group European Football Clubs (EFC) to operate the Club World Cup that is likely to see more Premier Lea
summary: Fifa has agreed to create a joint venture with the lobby group European Football Clubs (EFC) to operate the Club World Cup that is likely to see more Premier League clubs enter the lucrative competition. Chelsea earned around £84m from winning last year’s inaugural 32-team tournament, leading other big European clubs to lobby Fifa for it to be expanded to increase their chances of qualification. EFC coming on board is likely to accelerate plans to expand the competition to 48 clubs when it next takes place in 2029.

title: Maseko strike sends South Africa into first World Cup knockouts with win over South Korea
excerpt: South ​Africa celebrated a first World Cup knockout berth with jubilant scenes on Wednesday after Thapelo Maseko’s second-half strike secured a 1-0 victory over South ⁠Korea, who still have a chance of advancing as one of the
summary: South ​Africa celebrated a first World Cup knockout berth with jubilant scenes on Wednesday after Thapelo Maseko’s second-half strike secured a 1-0 victory over South ⁠Korea, who still have a chance of advancing as one of the eight best third-placed teams. Maseko fired into the bottom corner in the 63rd minute to put ⁠South Africa – who will face co-hosts Canada in Los Angeles on 28 June – on four points behind ​Group A winners Mexico, who beat the ‌Czechia 3-0. South Korea, ‌who started with talisman Son Heung-min on the bench, have three points. South Africa had failed to ‌advance from the group stage in their first three World Cup appearances in 1998, 2002 and 2010.

title: Fidalgo caps Mexico rout as co-hosts maintain 100% record and send Czechia out
excerpt: There are ways of leaving a tournament. You can go out with a sheen of glory, having gone to-to-toe with a great opponent. You can be unlucky and go home raging at referees and the fates. You can self-immolate in a blaze of red cards or own goals or spectacular errors. Or you can slink away witho
summary: There are ways of leaving a tournament. You can go out with a sheen of glory, having gone to-to-toe with a great opponent. You can be unlucky and go home raging at referees and the fates. You can self-immolate in a blaze of red cards or own goals or spectacular errors. Or you can slink away without leaving a trace – and that was the path followed by the Czechia. Nobody in 20 years will remember they were involved in this World Cup, other perhaps that Ireland fans reflecting on what a mess the Czechs made of the place they pinched from them in the playoff. A win would probably have taken Czechia through but that never looked likely. The Czechia coach Miroslav Koubek left out two of his most experienced campaigners in Patrik Schick and Tomas Soucek, and the way was left clear for a 17-year-old to control the game. Soucek did come on, but then landed awkwardly and left the field in obvious distress. There had been calls for Gilberto Mora to start both Mexico’s first two games of the tournament, and it was easy to see why. He had impressed even before playing a part in the first two Mexico goals.
